skill.name: agent-tools
skill.description: Run 150+ AI apps via inference.sh CLI - image generation, video creation, LLMs, search, 3D, Twitter automation. Models: FLUX, Veo, Gemini, Grok, Claude, Seedance, OmniHuman, Tavily, Exa, OpenRouter, and many more. Use when running AI apps, generating images/videos, calling LLMs, web search, or automating Twitter. Triggers: inference.sh, infsh, ai model, run ai, serverless ai, ai api, flux, veo, claude api, image generation, video generation, openrouter, tavily, exa search, twitter api, grok
skill.summary: Access 150+ cloud AI apps through inference.sh CLI - generate images, videos, 3D models, run LLMs, search the web, and automate Twitter from Claude Code
skill.author: toolshell

security_audit.summary: All 194 static analysis findings are false positives. This is a documentation-only skill containing markdown files with code examples. The skill itself does not execute any commands, make network requests, or access environment variables. All detected patterns are within markdown code blocks demonstrating how to use the inference.sh CLI tool. The allowed-tools restriction (Bash(infsh *)) further limits execution to infsh CLI commands only.

content.user_title: Run 150+ Cloud AI Apps from Claude Code
content.value_statement: Access 150+ AI models without local GPU. Generate images with FLUX, create videos with Veo, run Claude/Gemini LLMs, search the web with Tavily, and automate Twitter - all through a simple CLI that Claude Code can invoke.
actual_capabilities: Generate images using FLUX, Gemini, Grok Imagine, and Seedream models
Create videos with Veo 3.1, Seedance, Wan 2.5, and OmniHuman
Run LLMs including Claude, Gemini, Kimi, and any OpenRouter model
Perform web searches using Tavily and Exa search engines
Automate Twitter/X actions (post tweets, send DMs, follow users)
limitations: Requires internet connection to access cloud AI services
Must authenticate with inference.sh before running apps
Some apps may have rate limits or usage costs
Cannot run models locally - all processing happens in the cloud

use_cases.title: Generate AI Images for Content
use_cases.description: Create custom images for articles, social media, or presentations using FLUX or Gemini image models directly from Claude Code

use_cases.title: Prototype AI Video Workflows
use_cases.description: Test and generate AI videos using Veo or Seedance models without setting up local GPU infrastructure

use_cases.title: Build AI-Powered Automation
use_cases.description: Integrate multiple AI capabilities (LLMs, search, image generation) into automated workflows using Claude Code as the orchestration layer

prompt_templates.title: Generate an Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate an image of [describe subject] using FLUX model
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick image generation for any subject

prompt_templates.title: Create a Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a video showing [describe scene] using Veo 3.1 model

prompt_templates.title: Run LLM with Context
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Claude to analyze this text: [paste text]. Provide a summary and key insights.

prompt_templates.title: Web Search and Summarize
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the web for [topic] and summarize the top 5 most relevant results

output_examples.input: Generate an image of a cat astronaut floating in space
output_examples.output: Image generated successfully and saved to current directory. The image shows a cat wearing a spacesuit floating against a starry space background with Earth visible in the distance.

output_examples.input: Create a 5-second video of a drone flying over mountains at sunset
output_examples.output: Video generation task submitted. Task ID: abc123. Use 'infsh task get abc123' to check progress. Estimated completion time: 2-3 minutes.

best_practices: Always test apps with simple inputs first to understand their behavior before integrating into workflows
Use the sample input generator (infsh app sample) to understand required parameters for each app
Check task status asynchronously for long-running operations like video generation
Keep your API key secure by using environment variables instead of hardcoded values
anti_patterns: Don't generate videos or 3D models in interactive sessions - use the --no-wait flag and check status later
Avoid running apps without understanding their input schema - use 'infsh app get' to review parameters first
Never share your inference.sh API key or include it in prompts or code
Don't assume all apps are free - some may have usage costs or rate limits

faq.question: Do I need a GPU to use these AI apps?
faq.answer: No GPU required. All processing happens in the cloud through inference.sh. You only need the CLI tool and an internet connection.

faq.question: Is this skill free to use?
faq.answer: The skill itself is free, but some AI apps on inference.sh may have usage costs. Check the app details for pricing information.

faq.question: How do I authenticate?
faq.answer: Run 'infsh login' to authenticate via browser. For CI/CD, set the INFSH_API_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: Can I run multiple apps at once?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the --no-wait flag to run apps asynchronously, then check task status later with 'infsh task get'.

faq.question: What AI models are available?
faq.answer: Over 150 apps including FLUX, Veo, Claude, Gemini, Grok, Tavily, Exa, and many more. Use 'infsh app list' to see all options.

faq.question: Is my data private?
faq.answer: Data privacy depends on the specific app you use. Review the privacy policy for each AI model provider on inference.sh.
